ddls:
CREATE TABLE "computing_tasks" (
  "task_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each computing task */,
  "task_name" TEXT /* Name of the computing task */,
  "total_time" REAL /* Total time taken by the task */,
  "execution_time" REAL /* Execution time of the task */,
  "simd_width" INTEGER /* SIMD width used by the task */,
  "peak_eu_threads_occupancy" REAL /* Peak EU threads occupancy of the task */,
  "eu_threads_occupancy" REAL /* EU threads occupancy of the task */,
  "simd_utilization" REAL /* SIMD utilization of the task */,
  "memory_usage" REAL /* Memory usage of the task */,
  "cpu_utilization" REAL /* CPU utilization of the task */,
  "io_operations" INTEGER /* Number of I/O operations performed by the task */,
  "energy_consumption" REAL /* Energy consumption of the task */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("task_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "clusters" (
  "cluster_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each cluster */,
  "cluster_name" TEXT /* Name of the cluster */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the cluster */,
  "location" TEXT /* Physical location of the cluster */,
  "hardware_description" TEXT /* Description of the cluster's hardware */,
  "software_description" TEXT /* Description of the cluster's software */,
  "interconnect_type" TEXT /* Type of interconnect used in the cluster */,
  "storage_capacity" REAL /* Total storage capacity of the cluster */,
  "memory_capacity" REAL /* Total memory capacity of the cluster */,
  "cpu_capacity" REAL /* Total CPU capacity of the cluster */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("cluster_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "task_execution" (
  "execution_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each task execution */,
  "task_id" INTEGER /* ID of the computing task being executed */,
  "cluster_id" INTEGER /* ID of the cluster where the task is executed */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Start time of the task execution */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* End time of the task execution */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the task execution (e.g., running, completed, failed) */,
  "exit_code" INTEGER /* Exit code of the task execution */,
  "error_message" TEXT /* Error message if the task execution failed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("execution_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_task_execution_task_id FOREIGN KEY ("task_id") REFERENCES computing_tasks ("task_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_task_execution_cluster_id FOREIGN KEY ("cluster_id") REFERENCES clusters ("cluster_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., administrator, researcher, developer) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "organization" TEXT /* Organization of the user */,
  "location" TEXT /* Physical location of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "task_id" INTEGER /* ID of the computing task being accessed */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Date and time when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., read, write) */,
  "query_type" TEXT /* Type of query (e.g., SQL, API) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_task_id FOREIGN KEY ("task_id") REFERENCES computing_tasks ("task_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "task_id" INTEGER /* ID of the computing task related to the notification */,
  "cluster_id" INTEGER /* ID of the cluster where the task was executed */,
  "notification_time" TEXT /* Date and time when the notification was sent */,
  "message" TEXT /* Message describing the performance issue or resource constraint */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., email, SMS) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_task_id FOREIGN KEY ("task_id") REFERENCES computing_tasks ("task_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_cluster_id FOREIGN KEY ("cluster_id") REFERENCES clusters ("cluster_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role to which the permission belongs */,
  "permission_name" TEXT /* Name of the permission */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the permission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_permissions_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user to whom the role is assigned */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role assigned to the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "cluster_resources" (
  "resource_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each resource */,
  "cluster_id" INTEGER /* ID of the cluster to which the resource belongs */,
  "resource_name" TEXT /* Name of the resource */,
  "resource_type" TEXT /* Type of the resource (e.g., CPU, memory, storage) */,
  "usage" REAL /* Usage of the resource (e.g., percentage, GB) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("resource_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_cluster_resources_cluster_id FOREIGN KEY ("cluster_id") REFERENCES clusters ("cluster_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "system_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metric */,
  "cluster_id" INTEGER /* ID of the cluster to which the metric belongs */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the metric */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the metric */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of the metric (e.g., GHz, GB/s) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_system_metrics_cluster_id FOREIGN KEY ("cluster_id") REFERENCES clusters ("cluster_id")
)
